news.title: Iran vows to kill Israel's Netanyahu as impact of war on Gulf region widens
news.summary: Oil loading reportedly resumes in the UAE's Fujairah port following a fire. Israel says it has killed two senior Iranian intelligence officials as oil loading resumes.

news.title: Price caps, taking the stairs, and short-sleeved shirts: How countries are coping with the Iran war energy shock
news.summary: Some countries have imposed price caps and released oil stockpiles to maintain energy supplies. Others have tried to reduce consumption by imposing price caps or releasing stockpiles.

news.title: Global week ahead: Price pressure in the pipeline
news.summary: Keep inflation under control becomes a critical task for central banks. Keeping inflation undercontrol becomes a key task for Central Bank governors. The U.S. Federal Reserve is the world's largest central bank, with an interest rate of nearly zero.

news.title: Fertitta in weekend deal talks to acquire Caesars, while billionaire Carl Icahn waits in the wings
news.summary: The deal is not expected to close until 2027. The deal would not be finalized until early April, if it gets done at all.

news.title: FCC chair slams broadcasters after Trump disputes reports on Iran-damaged U.S. tankers
news.summary: FCC Chair Brendan Carr blasted broadcasters shortly after President Donald Trump called reports that Iran struck five U.S. tanker planes "fake news"

news.title: ‘I am stuck in a low-income trap’: I’m a teacher and very good at my job. Will I ever earn six figures?
news.summary: “My low salary requires me to have multiple other part-time jobs,” she says. “I’m not able to work full-time. I’ve got to work part time.”

news.title: ‘Healthcare is important to me’: I’m worried about relocating to Florida from New Jersey. Are hospitals there under stress?
news.summary: “The issue is not the weather, high insurance premiums or high maintenance fees,” he says. “It’s the cost of living.”

news.title: ‘I’m experiencing issues with arthritis’: I’m 68 with $3 million saved. Why am I not ready for a life of leisure?
news.summary: “I can draw $4,300 a month in Social Security if I retire this summer,” he says. “I’ll be able to retire in the summer.”

news.title: ‘I find that advice questionable’: Is it time to rethink the rule of tapping your Roth last — after your 401(k) and IRA?
news.summary: “I’ve read this advice again and again,” he says. “I've read it over and over again.”

news.title: ‘I didn’t ask a man to rear-end my car’: Social Security is replacing my disability benefits. Will the fund run out of money?
news.summary: “I didn’t ask a man to rear-end my car at a red light,” she said. “I’m not a bad person. I’ve always been a good person.”
